title: fast-xml-parser Configuration, Entity Limits, Depth, and Dangerous Keys · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers fast-xml-parser configuration, entity limits, depth, and dangerous keys into a changed hardened bounded XML parse adapter. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of hardened bounded XML parse adapter with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress th…
objectives: Enforce wire, decoded, character, entity, expansion, nesting, attribute, item, text, and duration budgets before and during parsing.
Configure namespaces, attributes, text nodes, value coercion, arrays, order, entities, dangerous properties, allowed tags, and validation deliberately.
Reject or tightly bound DOCTYPE and custom entities using current max-count, depth, total-expansion, expanded-length, and external-reference policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Xml Byte Depth Limits
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1101 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove bounded XML options, entities, depth, dangerous-key rejection, and causal failures; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce wire, decoded, character, entity, expansion, nesting, attribute, item, text, and duration budgets before and during parsing.
